definition: The indefinite continued progress of existence and events in the past, present, and future regarded as a whole.
examples: I don't have enough time to finish this.
What time is it?
fun_fact: Time flies when you're having fun!

definition: Activity involving mental or physical effort done in order to achieve a purpose or result.
examples: I have a lot of work to do.
She leaves for work at 8 AM.
fun_fact: The word 'work' is one of the most common verbs in the English language.

definition: Human beings in general or considered collectively.
examples: Many people like chocolate.
The people in this town are very friendly.
fun_fact: The singular of 'people' can be 'person', but 'peoples' refers to distinct ethnic groups.

definition: A method, style, or manner of doing something.
examples: This is the best way to learn English.
Can you show me the way to the station?
fun_fact: 'Way' idiomatically means 'much' (e.g., 'way better').

definition: Of great significance or value; likely to have a profound effect on success, survival, or well-being.
examples: It is important to eat healthy food.
She is an important member of the team.
fun_fact: The opposite is 'unimportant'.

definition: Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment.
examples: I am so happy to see you.
They had a happy marriage.
fun_fact: 'Happiest' is the superlative form.

definition: A group of one or more parents and their children living together as a unit.
examples: I love my family.
We are having a family dinner tonight.
fun_fact: The term can be singular or plural depending on whether you view them as a unit or individuals.

definition: The condition that distinguishes animals and plants from inorganic matter, including the capacity for growth, reproduction, functional activity, and continual change preceding death.
examples: Life is beautiful.
He risked his life to save her.
fun_fact: 'C'est la vie' is a common French phrase meaning 'that's life'.

definition: A period of twenty-four hours as a unit of time, reckoned from one midnight to the next, corresponding to a rotation of the earth on its axis.
examples: Have a nice day!
I study English every day.
fun_fact: 'Daytime' refers to the time it is light outside.

definition: The earth, together with all of its countries, peoples, and natural features.
examples: He wants to travel the world.
It's a small world.
fun_fact: The world encompasses everything known to us.

definition: To be desired or approved of.
examples: This is a good book.
You did a good job.
fun_fact: 'Better' and 'best' are the irregular comparative and superlative forms.

definition: A written or printed work consisting of pages glued or sewn together along one side and bound in covers.
examples: I am reading a fascinating book.
She wrote a book about her travels.
fun_fact: The longest sentence ever printed in a book is 823 words long (in Victor Hugo's Les Misérables).

definition: Gain or acquire knowledge of or skill in (something) by study, experience, or being taught.
examples: I want to learn how to swim.
She learns quickly.
fun_fact: The brain never stops learning.

definition: The principal method of human communication, consisting of words used in a structured and conventional way and conveyed by speech, writing, or gesture.
examples: English is a global language.
Learning a new language is challenging but rewarding.
fun_fact: There are over 7,000 languages spoken in the world today.

definition: At all times; on all occasions.
examples: I always brush my teeth.
She is always late.
fun_fact: An adverb of frequency.

definition: At no time in the past or future; on no occasion; not ever.
examples: I have never been to Paris.
Never say never.
fun_fact: Often used in double negatives informally, though grammatically incorrect.

definition: A person whom one knows and with whom one has a bond of mutual affection.
examples: She is my best friend.
I made some new friends at school.
fun_fact: Friendship is essential for mental health.

definition: A colorless, transparent, odorless liquid that forms the seas, lakes, rivers, and rain and is the basis of the fluids of living organisms.
examples: I drink a lot of water every day.
The water is freezing.
fun_fact: About 71% of the Earth's surface is water-covered.

definition: Any nutritious substance that people or animals eat or drink, or that plants absorb, in order to maintain life and growth.
examples: I love Italian food.
We need to buy some food for the party.
fun_fact: The most consumed food in the world is rice.

definition: A current medium of exchange in the form of coins and banknotes; coins and banknotes collectively.
examples: He earns a lot of money.
Money can't buy happiness.
fun_fact: The first paper money was created in China.
